original_query: select p_brand, p_type, p_size, count(distinct ps_suppkey) as supplier_cnt from partsupp, part where p_partkey = ps_partkey and p_brand <> 'Brand#43' and p_type not like 'PROMO PLATED%' and p_size in (18, 8, 33, 17, 27, 6, 1, 50) and ps_suppkey not in ( select s_suppkey from supplier where s_comment like '%Customer%Complaints%' ) group by p_brand, p_type, p_size order by supplier_cnt desc, p_brand, p_type, p_size;
rewritten_query:
SELECT p_brand, p_type, p_size, COUNT(DISTINCT ps_suppkey) AS supplier_cnt
FROM partsupp
JOIN part ON p_partkey = ps_partkey
LEFT JOIN supplier s ON partsupp.ps_suppkey = s.s_suppkey AND s.s_comment LIKE '%Customer%Complaints%'
WHERE p_brand <> 'Brand#43'
  AND p_type NOT LIKE 'PROMO PLATED%'
  AND p_size IN (18, 8, 33, 17, 27, 6, 1, 50)
  AND s.s_suppkey IS NULL
GROUP BY p_brand, p_type, p_size
ORDER BY supplier_cnt DESC, p_brand, p_type, p_size;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replace NOT IN subquery with LEFT JOIN and IS NULL check for potentially better performance
Use explicit JOIN syntax instead of comma-separated tables for better readability and potentially better query planning
